folio.label: 26v
folio.l: There as Mars , hath his souäeigne mansiou~
First on the wal , was peynted a forest
In whicú there dwelleth , neiæä man ne best
With knotty knarry , barreyne trees olde
Of stubbes sharpe , and hedous to byholde
In whicú there ran , a rombel in a swougú
As though a storme , shulde bresten euäy bougú
And dounward from an hille , vnder a bent
There stode a temple , of Mars Armypotent
Wrought al of borned stele , of which æe entree
Was long and streyt , and gastly for to se
And æäe ou©t cam a rage , and swicú a voisve©evois ve©e
That it mad al the gate , for to reysresereys rese
The northern ligút , in at the dores shone
For wyndowe on the wal , ne was æäe none
Thorugú whicú men mygúten , eny ligút discerne
The dore was al , of Athamant et äne
I clenched ouätwertouäthwertouäthwert, and endlongö
With Iren tougú , and for to make it strongö
Euäy piller , the temple to sustene
Was to~ne gret of Iren , brigút & shene
¢ There saw I first , the derk ymagenynge
Of felonye , and al the compassynge
The cruel Ire , red as eny glede
The pyke purs , and eke the pale drede
The smylere w æe knyf , vnder the cloke
The shippen brennyng , w the blak smoke

note.notetxt: brennyng1142there is always a little tail added to the g in final position, but in the transcription it is not always indicated.ODR

folio.l: The tresou~ of the morderyng , in the bedde
The open werre , w woundes al be bledde
Conteke with blody knyf , and sharp~ manace
Al ful of chidyng , was this sory place
The sleere of hi~ self , ©et saugú I there
His hert blode , hath bathed al his heere
The nail I dreuen , in the shode a nygút
The colde detú , w mouth gapyng vp rigút
¢ A myddes of the temple , sat myschaunce
With discomfort , & sory countenaunce
ñet saugh I woodnes , laughyng in his rage
Armed , compleynt , ou©t hees , & fixxxfiersfixxxersoutrage
The careyne in æe bussú , w throte koruen
A , M&lbar; , sleyn , and nou©t of qualme storuen
The teraunt with the pray , by force I raft´

note.notetxt: with1157 the h isnot very well executed: double check against manuscriptODR

folio.l: The Toun destroied , æäe was no æing I laft´
ñet saugh I brent , the shippes hoppesteres
The honte strangelid , with the wylde beres
